revision.text:
'''宇佐風土記の丘'''（うさふどきのおか）は、[[文化庁]]の[[風土記の丘]]設置構想に基づき、[[1981年]]（昭和56年）に[[大分県]][[宇佐市]]大字高森字京塚に開設された[[野外博物館]]・史跡[[公園]]である。
== 概要 == 九州最古の[[前方後円墳]]である赤塚古墳をはじめとするの6基の前方後円墳及びその周囲の古墳群からなり、国の[[史跡]]に指定されている[[川部・高森古墳群]]を中心とした総面積19.2haの大規模な史跡公園で、宇佐市を南から北に縦貫する[[駅館川]]右岸の台地上に位置している。
敷地内には、その中心となる展示・研究施設として、'''[[大分県立歴史博物館]]'''が開設されている。この博物館は、宇佐風土記の丘の開設と同時に大分県立宇佐風土記の丘歴史民俗資料館として開館し、1998年（平成10年）に常設展示館の増設等を行なうとともに大分県立歴史博物館に改称して、現在に至っている。
また、園内各所には、四季の花木が植栽されており、特に、4月の桜と6月の菖蒲は、名所として有名である。
== 関連項目 == * [[大分県立歴史博物館]] * [[川部・高森古墳群]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://rekisihakubutukan-b.oita-ed.jp/rekihaku/usafudoki.html 史跡公園「宇佐風土記の丘」]
{{DEFAULTSORT:うさふときのおか}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:大分県の古墳]] [[Category:大分県の史跡]] [[Category:宇佐市]]

{{Park |name = Usa Fudoki no Oka |english_name = Usa Fudoki no Oka Park |image = Oita Prefectural Museum of History in Usa city, Oita prefecture.JPG |image_caption = Main facility: {{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|ja|大分県立歴史博物館}} |country = Japan |city = {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, {{ill|Usa, Ōita|ja|宇佐市}} |latitude_deg = 33 |latitude_min = 32 |latitude_sec = 32.3 |longitude_deg = 131 |longitude_min = 21 |longitude_sec = 36.9 |map = Japan Oita |type = Historic Site Park |area = 19.2 hectares{{Cite web|ja|author=Usa City Official Tourism Site|url=https://www.city.usa.oita.jp/tourist/touristspot/touristspot2/touristspot3/10184.html|title=Ōita Prefectural Museum of History and Fudoki no Oka|work=Usa City|accessdate=2022-05-02}} |former_name = Ōita Prefectural Usa Fudoki no Oka Historical Folklore Museum |established = 1981 (Shōwa 56) |operator = Ōita Prefecture |parking = 150 cars, 13 large buses |headquarters = {{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|ja|大分県立歴史博物館}} |headquarters_address = Takamori, Usa City, Kyōzuka {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|32|32.3|N|131|21|36.9|E}}}} |access = About 10 minutes by car from JR Usa Station
About 5 minutes by car from JR Yanagigaura Station
About 5 minutes by car from JR Buzen-Nagasu Station |official_site = [https://www.pref.oita.jp/site/rekishihakubutsukan/ Ōita Prefectural Board of Education Official Page] }} '''Usa Fudoki no Oka''' (宇佐風土記の丘) is an {{ill|open-air museum|ja|野外博物館}} and historic site {{ill|park|ja|公園}} located in Kyōzuka, Takamori, {{ill|Usa, Ōita|ja|宇佐市}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, established in 1981 (Shōwa 56) based on the {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|ja|文化庁}}'s plan for creating "Fudoki no Oka." == Overview == The park covers an extensive area of 19.2 hectares, centered around the {{ill|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|ja|川部・高森古墳群}}, a nationally designated {{ill|historic site|ja|史跡}} that includes six keyhole-shaped burial mounds, notably the oldest such mound in Kyushu, the Akatsuka Kofun, along with surrounding burial mounds. It is situated on a plateau on the right bank of the {{ill|Ekigawa River|ja|駅館川}}, which runs north-south through Usa City. Within the park is the central exhibit and research facility, the '''{{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|ja|大分県立歴史博物館}}'''. This museum was originally established as the Ōita Prefectural Usa Fudoki no Oka Historical Folklore Museum at the same time as the park’s opening. In 1998 (Heisei 10), it underwent expansion to include a permanent exhibition hall and was renamed the Ōita Prefectural Museum of History, which it remains today. Additionally, various seasonal flowers and trees are planted throughout the park, with cherry blossoms in April and irises in June being particularly famous as scenic highlights. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|ja|大分県立歴史博物館}} * {{ill|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|ja|川部・高森古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://rekisihakubutukan-b.oita-ed.jp/rekihaku/usafudoki.html Historic Site Park "Usa Fudoki no Oka"] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Parks in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Usa, Ōita]] [[Category:History of Usa, Ōita]] [[ja:宇佐風土記の丘]] [[ja:%E5%AE%87%E4%BD%90%E9%A2%A8%E5%9C%9F%E8%A8%98%E3%81%AE%E4%B8%98]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Usa Fudoki Hill]][[Category:kk]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Usa Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:unsorted completion categories]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:redirect]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Usa Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Usa Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Usa Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]]

{{Park |name = Usa Fudoki no Oka |english_name = Usa Fudoki no Oka Park |image = Oita Prefectural Museum of History in Usa city, Oita prefecture.JPG |image_caption = Main facility: {{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|ja|大分県立歴史博物館}} |country = Japan |city = {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, {{ill|Usa, Ōita|ja|宇佐市}} |latitude_deg = 33 |latitude_min = 32 |latitude_sec = 32.3 |longitude_deg = 131 |longitude_min = 21 |longitude_sec = 36.9 |map = Japan Oita |type = Historic Site Park |area = 19.2 hectares{{Cite web|ja|author=Usa City Official Tourism Site|url=https://www.city.usa.oita.jp/tourist/touristspot/touristspot2/touristspot3/10184.html|title=Ōita Prefectural Museum of History and Fudoki no Oka|work=Usa City|accessdate=2022-05-02}} |former_name = Ōita Prefectural Usa Fudoki no Oka Historical Folklore Museum |established = 1981 (Shōwa 56) |operator = Ōita Prefecture |parking = 150 cars, 13 large buses |headquarters = {{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|ja|大分県立歴史博物館}} |headquarters_address = Takamori, Usa City, Kyōzuka {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|32|32.3|N|131|21|36.9|E}}}} |access = About 10 minutes by car from JR Usa Station

It is situated on a plateau on the right bank of the {{ill|Ekigawa River|ja|駅館川}}, which runs north-south through Usa City. {{Infobox park |name = Usa Fudoki no Oka |english_name = Usa Fudoki no Oka Park |image = Oita Prefectural Museum of History in Usa city, Oita prefecture.JPG |image_caption = Main facility: {{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|ja|大分県立歴史博物館}} |country = Japan |city = {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, {{ill|Usa, Ōita|ja|宇佐市}} |latitude_deg = 33 |latitude_min = 32 |latitude_sec = 32.3 |longitude_deg = 131 |longitude_min = 21 |longitude_sec = 36.9 |map = Japan Oita |type = Historic Site Park |area = 19.2 hectares{{Cite web|ja|author=Usa City Official Tourism Site|url=https://www.city.usa.oita.jp/tourist/touristspot/touristspot2/touristspot3/10184.html|title=Ōita Prefectural Museum of History and Fudoki no Oka|work=Usa City|accessdate=2022-05-02}} |former_name = Ōita Prefectural Usa Fudoki no Oka Historical Folklore Museum |established = 1981 (Shōwa 56) |operator = Ōita Prefecture |parking = 150 cars, 13 large buses |headquarters = {{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|ja|大分県立歴史博物館}} |headquarters_address = Takamori, Usa City, Kyōzuka {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|32|32.3|N|131|21|36.9|E}}}} |access = About 10 minutes by car from JR Usa Station
About 5 minutes by car from JR Yanagigaura Station
About 5 minutes by car from JR Buzen-Nagasu Station |official_site = [https://www.pref.oita.jp/site/rekishihakubutsukan/ Ōita Prefectural Board of Education Official Page] }} '''Usa Fudoki no Oka''' (宇佐風土記の丘) is an {{ill|open-air museum|ja|野外博物館}} and historic site {{ill|park|ja|公園}} located in Kyōzuka, Takamori, {{ill|Usa, Ōita|ja|宇佐市}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, established in 1981 (Shōwa 56) based on the {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|ja|文化庁}}'s plan for creating "Fudoki no Oka." == Overview == The park covers an extensive area of 19.2 hectares, centered around the {{ill|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|ja|川部・高森古墳群}}, a nationally designated {{ill|historic site|ja|史跡}} that includes six keyhole-shaped burial mounds, notably the oldest such mound in Kyushu, the Akatsuka Kofun, along with surrounding burial mounds. It is situated on a plateau on the right bank of the {{ill|Ekigawa River|ja|駅館川}}, which runs north-south through Usa City. Within the park is the central exhibit and research facility, the '''{{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|ja|大分県立歴史博物館}}'''. This museum was originally established as the Ōita Prefectural Usa Fudoki no Oka Historical Folklore Museum at the same time as the park’s opening. In 1998 (Heisei 10), it underwent expansion to include a permanent exhibition hall and was renamed the Ōita Prefectural Museum of History, which it remains today.https://www.city.usa.oita.jp/tourist/touristspot/touristspot2/touristspot3/10184.html Additionally, various seasonal flowers and trees are planted throughout the park, with cherry blossoms in April and irises in June being particularly famous as scenic highlights. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|ja|大分県立歴史博物館}} * {{ill|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|ja|川部・高森古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://rekisihakubutukan-b.oita-ed.jp/rekihaku/usafudoki.html Historic Site Park "Usa Fudoki no Oka"] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Parks in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Usa, Ōita]] [[Category:History of Usa, Ōita]] [[ja:宇佐風土記の丘]] [[ja:%E5%AE%87%E4%BD%90%E9%A2%A8%E5%9C%9F%E8%A8%98%E3%81%AE%E4%B8%98]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Usa Fudoki Hill-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Usa Fudoki Hill]][[Category:kk]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Usa Fudoki Hill]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Usa Fudoki Hill-qq]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Fudoki Hills]] [[en:Usa Fudoki Hill]][[en:Usa Fudoki Hill]][[en:Usa Fudoki Hill]]

About 5 minutes by car from JR Buzen-Nagasu Station |official_site = [https://www.pref.oita.jp/site/rekishihakubutsukan/ Ōita Prefectural Board of Education Official Page] }} '''Usa Fudoki no Oka''' (宇佐風土記の丘) is an {{ill|open-air museum|ja|野外博物館}} and historic site {{ill|park|ja|公園}} located in Kyōzuka, Takamori, {{ill|Usa, Ōita|ja|宇佐市}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, established in 1981 (Shōwa 56) based on the {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|ja|文化庁}}'s plan for creating "Fudoki no Oka." == Overview == The park covers an extensive area of 19.2 hectares, centered around the {{ill|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|ja|川部・高森古墳群}}, a nationally designated {{ill|historic site|ja|史跡}} that includes six keyhole-shaped burial mounds, notably the oldest such mound in Kyushu, the Akatsuka Kofun, along with surrounding burial mounds. It is situated on a plateau on the right bank of the {{ill|Ekigawa River|ja|駅館川}}, which runs north-south through Usa City. Within the park is the central exhibit and research facility, the '''{{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|ja|大分県立歴史博物館}}'''. This museum was originally established as the Ōita Prefectural Usa Fudoki no Oka Historical Folklore Museum at the same time as the park’s opening. In 1998 (Heisei 10), it underwent expansion to include a permanent exhibition hall and was renamed the Ōita Prefectural Museum of History, which it remains today.https://www.city.usa.oita.jp/tourist/touristspot/touristspot2/touristspot3/10184.html Additionally, various seasonal flowers and trees are planted throughout the park, with cherry blossoms in April and irises in June being particularly famous as scenic highlights. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|ja|大分県立歴史博物館}} * {{ill|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|ja|川部・高森古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://rekisihakubutukan-b.oita-ed.jp/rekihaku/usafudoki.html Historic Site Park "Usa Fudoki no Oka"] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Parks in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Usa, Ōita]] [[Category:History of Usa, Ōita]] [[ja:宇佐風土記の丘]] [[ja:%E5%AE%87%E4%BD%90%E9%A2%A8%E5%9C%9F%E8%A8%98%E3%81%AE%E4%B8%98]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Usa Fudoki Hill-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Usa Fudoki Hill]][[Category:kk]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Usa Fudoki Hill]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Usa Fudoki Hill-qq]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Fudoki Hills]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Usa Fudoki Hill}][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]][[sn:Usa Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Usa Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Usa Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Usa Fudoki Hill]][[en:Usa Fudoki Hill]][[en:Usa Fudoki Hill]]

About 5 minutes by car from JR Buzen-Nagasu Station |official_site = [https://www.pref.oita.jp/site/rekishihakubutsukan/ Ōita Prefectural Board of Education Official Page] }} '''Usa Fudoki no Oka''' (宇佐風土記の丘) is an {{ill|open-air museum|ja|野外博物館}} and historic site {{ill|park|ja|公園}} located in Kyōzuka, Takamori, {{ill|Usa, Ōita|ja|宇佐市}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, established in 1981 (Shōwa 56) based on the {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|ja|文化庁}}'s plan for creating "Fudoki no Oka." == Overview == The park covers an extensive area of 19.2 hectares, centered around the {{ill|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|ja|川部・高森古墳群}}, a nationally designated {{ill|historic site|ja|史跡}} that includes six keyhole-shaped burial mounds, notably the oldest such mound in Kyushu, the Akatsuka Kofun, along with surrounding burial mounds. It is situated on a plateau on the right bank of the {{ill|Ekigawa River|ja|駅館川}}, which runs north-south through Usa City. Within the park is the central exhibit and research facility, the '''{{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|ja|大分県立歴史博物館}}'''. This museum was originally established as the Ōita Prefectural Usa Fudoki no Oka Historical Folklore Museum at the same time as the park’s opening. In 1998 (Heisei 10), it underwent expansion to include a permanent exhibition hall and was renamed the Ōita Prefectural Museum of History, which it remains today.https://www.city.usa.oita.jp/tourist/touristspot/touristspot2/touristspot3/10184.html Additionally, various seasonal flowers and trees are planted throughout the park, with cherry blossoms in April and irises in June being particularly famous as scenic highlights. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|ja|大分県立歴史博物館}} * today.https://www.city.usa.oita.jp/tourist/touristspot/touristspot2/touristspot3/10184.html Additionally, various seasonal flowers and trees are planted throughout the park, with cherry blossoms in {{Infobox park |name = Usa Fudoki no Oka |english_name = Usa Fudoki no Oka Park |image = Oita Prefectural Museum of History in Usa city, Oita prefecture.JPG |image_caption = Main facility: {{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|ja|大分県立歴史博物館}} |country = Japan |city = {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, {{ill|Usa, Ōita|ja|宇佐市}} |latitude_deg = 33 |latitude_min = 32 |latitude_sec = 32.3 |longitude_deg = 131 |longitude_min = 21 |longitude_sec = 36.9 |map = Japan Oita |type = Historic Site Park |area = 19.2 hectares{{Cite web|ja|author=Usa City Official Tourism Site|url=https://www.city.usa.oita.jp/tourist/touristspot/touristspot2/touristspot3/10184.html|title=Ōita Prefectural Museum of History and Fudoki no Oka|work=Usa City|accessdate=2022-05-02}} |former_name = Ōita Prefectural Usa Fudoki no Oka Historical Folklore Museum |established = 1981 (Shōwa 56) |operator = Ōita Prefecture |parking = 150 cars, 13 large buses |headquarters = {{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|ja|大分県立歴史博物館}} |headquarters_address = Takamori, Usa City, Kyōzuka {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|32|32.3|N|131|21|36.9|E}}}} |access = About 10 minutes by car from JR Usa Station
About 5 minutes by car from JR Yanagigaura Station
About 5 minutes by car from JR Buzen-Nagasu Station |official_site = [https://www.pref.oita.jp/site/rekishihakubutsukan/ Ōita Prefectural Board of Education Official Page] }} '''Usa Fudoki no Oka''' (宇佐風土記の丘) is an {{ill|open-air museum|ja|野外博物館}} and historic site {{ill|park|ja|公園}} located in Kyōzuka, Takamori, {{ill|Usa, Ōita|ja|宇佐市}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, established in 1981 (Shōwa 56) based on the {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|ja|文化庁}}'s plan for creating "Fudoki no Oka." == Overview == The park covers an extensive area of 19.2 hectares, centered around the {{ill|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|ja|川部・高森古墳群}}, a nationally designated {{ill|historic site|ja|史跡}} that includes six keyhole-shaped burial mounds, notably the oldest such mound in Kyushu, the Akatsuka Kofun, along with surrounding burial mounds. It is situated on a plateau on the right bank of the {{ill|Ekigawa River|ja|駅館川}}, which runs north-south through Usa City. Within the park is the central exhibit and research facility, the '''{{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|ja|大分県立歴史博物館}}'''. This museum was originally established as the Ōita Prefectural Usa Fudoki no Oka Historical Folklore Museum at the same time as the park’s opening. In 1998 (Heisei 10), it underwent expansion to include a permanent exhibition hall and was renamed the Ōita Prefectural Museum of History, which it remains today.https://www.city.usa.oita.jp/tourist/touristspot/touristspot2/touristspot3/10184.html Additionally, various seasonal flowers and trees are planted throughout the park, with cherry blossoms in April and irises in June being particularly famous as scenic highlights. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|ja|大分県立歴史博物館}} * {{ill|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|ja|川部・高森古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://rekisihakubutukan-b.oita-ed.jp/rekihaku/usafudoki.html Historic Site Park "Usa Fudoki no Oka"] About 5 minutes by car from JR Buzen-Nagasu Station |official_site = [https://www.pref.oita.jp/site/rekishihakubutsukan/ Ōita Prefectural Board of Education Official Page] }} '''Usa Fudoki no Oka''' (宇佐風土記の丘) is an {{ill|open-air museum|ja|野外博物館}} and historic site {{ill|park|ja|公園}} located in Kyōzuka, Takamori, {{ill|Usa, Ōita|ja|宇佐市}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, established in 1981 (Shōwa 56) based on the {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|ja|文化庁}}'s plan for creating "Fudoki no Oka." == Overview == The park covers an extensive area of 19.2 hectares, centered around the {{ill|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|ja|川部・高森古墳群}}, a nationally designated {{ill|historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} that includes six keyhole-shaped burial mounds, notably the oldest such mound in Kyushu, the Akatsuka Kofun, along with surrounding burial mounds. It is situated on a plateau on the right bank of the {{ill|Ekigawa River|ja|駅館川}}, which runs north-south through Usa City. Within the park is the central exhibit and research facility, the '''{{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|ja|大分県立歴史博物館}}'''. This museum was originally established as the Ōita Prefectural Usa Fudoki no Oka Historical Folklore Museum at the same time as the park’s opening. In 1998 (Heisei 10), it underwent expansion to include a permanent exhibition hall and was renamed the Ōita Prefectural Museum of History, which it remains today.https://www.city.usa.oita.jp/tourist/touristspot/touristspot2/touristspot3/10184.html Additionally, various seasonal flowers and trees are planted throughout the park, with cherry blossoms in April and irises in June being particularly famous as scenic highlights. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|ja|大分県立歴史博物館}} * About 5 minutes by car from JR Yanagigaura Station
About 5 minutes by car from JR Buzen-Nagasu Station |official_site = [https://www.pref.oita.jp/site/rekishihakubutsukan/ Ōita Prefectural Board of Education Official Page] }} '''Usa Fudoki no Oka''' (宇佐風土記の丘) is an {{ill|open-air museum|ja|野外博物館}} and historic site {{ill|park|ja|公園}} located in Kyōzuka, Takamori, {{ill|Usa, Ōita|ja|宇佐市}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, established in 1981 (Shōwa 56) based on the {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|ja|文化庁}}'s plan for creating "Fudoki no Oka." == Overview == The park covers an extensive area of 19.2 hectares, centered around the {{ill|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|ja|川部・高森古墳群}}, a nationally designated {{ill|historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} that includes six keyhole-shaped burial mounds, notably the oldest such mound in Kyushu, the Akatsuka Kofun, along with surrounding burial mounds. It is situated on a plateau on the right bank of the {{ill|Ekigawa River|ja|駅館川}}, which runs north-south through Usa City. Within the park is the central exhibit and research facility, the '''{{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|ja|大分県立歴史博物館}}'''. This museum was originally established as the Ōita Prefectural Usa Fudoki no Oka Historical Folklore Museum at the same time as the park’s opening. In 1998 (Heisei 10), it underwent expansion to include a permanent exhibition hall and was renamed the Ōita Prefectural Museum of History, which it remains today.https://www.city.usa.oita.jp/tourist/touristspot/touristspot2/touristspot3/10184.html Additionally, various seasonal flowers and trees are planted throughout the park, with cherry blossoms in April and irises in June being particularly famous as scenic highlights. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|ja|大分県立歴史博物館}} * About 5 minutes by car from JR Yanagigaura Station
About 5 minutes by car from JR Buzen-Nagasu Station |official_site = [https://www.pref.oita.jp/site/rekishihakubutsukan/ Ōita Prefectural Board of Education Official Page] }} '''Usa Fudoki no Oka''' (宇佐風土記の丘) is an {{ill|open-air museum|ja|野外博物館}} and historic site {{ill|park|ja|公園}} located in Kyōzuka, Takamori, {{ill|Usa, Ōita|ja|宇佐市}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, established in 1981 (Shōwa 56) based on the {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|ja|文化庁}}'s plan for creating "Fudoki no Oka." == Overview == The park covers an extensive area of 19.2 hectares, centered around the {{ill|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|ja|川部・高森古墳群}}, a nationally designated {{ill|historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} that includes six keyhole-shaped burial mounds, notably the oldest such mound in Kyushu, the Akatsuka Kofun, along with surrounding burial mounds. It is situated on a plateau on the right bank of the {{ill|Ekigawa River|ja|駅館川}}, which runs north-south through Usa City. Within the park is the central exhibit and research facility, the '''{{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|ja|大分県立歴史博物館}}'''. This museum was originally established as the Ōita Prefectural Usa Fudoki no Oka Historical Folklore Museum at the same time as the park’s opening. In 1998 (Heisei 10), it underwent expansion to include a permanent exhibition hall and was renamed the Ōita Prefectural Museum of History, which it remains today.https://www.city.usa.oita.jp/tourist/touristspot/touristspot2/touristspot3/10184.html Additionally, various seasonal flowers and trees are planted throughout the park, with cherry blossoms in April and irises in June being particularly famous as scenic highlights. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|ja|大分県立歴史博物館}} * {{Infobox park |name = Usa Fudoki no Oka |english_name = Usa Fudoki no Oka Park |image = Oita Prefectural Museum of History in Usa city, Oita prefecture.JPG |image_caption = Main facility: {{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|ja|大分県立歴史博物館}} |country = Japan |city = {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, {{ill|Usa, Ōita|ja|宇佐市}} |latitude_deg = 33 |latitude_min = 32 |latitude_sec = 32.3 |longitude_deg = 131 |longitude_min = 21 |longitude_sec = 36.9 |map = Japan Oita |type = Historic Site Park |area = 19.2 hectares{{Cite web|ja|author=Usa City Official Tourism Site|url=https://www.city.usa.oita.jp/tourist/touristspot/touristspot2/touristspot3/10184.html|title=Ōita Prefectural Museum of History and Fudoki no Oka|work=Usa City|accessdate=2022-05-02}} |former_name = Ōita Prefectural Usa Fudoki no Oka Historical Folklore Museum |established = 1981 (Shōwa 56) |operator = Ōita Prefecture |parking = 150 cars, 13 large buses |headquarters = {{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|ja|大分県立歴史博物館}} |headquarters_address = Takamori, Usa City, Kyōzuka {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|32|32.3|N|131|21|36.9|E}}}} |access = About 10 minutes by car from JR Usa Station
About 5 minutes by car from JR Yanagigaura Station
About 5 minutes by car from JR Buzen-Nagasu Station |official_site = [https://www.pref.oita.jp/site/rekishihakubutsukan/ Ōita Prefectural Board of Education Official Page] }} '''Usa Fudoki no Oka''' (宇佐風土記の丘) is an {{ill|open-air museum|ja|野外博物館}} and historic site {{ill|park|ja|公園}} located in Kyōzuka, Takamori, {{ill|Usa, Ōita|ja|宇佐市}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, established in 1981 (Shōwa 56) based on the {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|ja|文化庁}}'s plan for creating "Fudoki no Oka." == Overview == The park covers an extensive area of 19.2 hectares, centered around the {{ill|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|ja|川部・高森古墳群}}, a nationally designated {{ill|historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} that includes six keyhole-shaped burial mounds, notably the oldest such mound in Kyushu, the Akatsuka Kofun, along with surrounding burial mounds. It is situated on a plateau on the right bank of the {{ill|Ekigawa River|ja|駅館川}}, which runs north-south through Usa City. Within the park is the central exhibit and research facility, the '''{{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|ja|大分県立歴史博物館}}'''. This museum was originally established as the Ōita Prefectural Usa Fudoki no Oka Historical Folklore Museum at the same time as the park’s opening. In 1998 (Heisei 10), it underwent expansion to include a permanent exhibition hall and was renamed the Ōita Prefectural Museum of History, which it remains today.https://www.city.usa.oita.jp/tourist/touristspot/touristspot2/touristspot3/10184.html Additionally, various seasonal flowers and trees are planted throughout the park, with cherry blossoms in April and irises in June being particularly famous as scenic highlights. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|ja|大分県立歴史博物館}} * About 5 minutes by car from JR Yanagigaura Station
About 5 minutes by car from JR Buzen-Nagasu Station |official_site = [https://www.pref.oita.jp/site/rekishihakubutsukan/ Ōita Prefectural Board of Education Official Page] }} '''Usa Fudoki no Oka''' (宇佐風土記の丘) is an {{ill|open-air museum|ja|野外博物館}} and historic site {{ill|park|ja|公園}} located in Kyōzuka, Takamori, {{ill|Usa, Ōita|ja|宇佐市}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, established in 1981 (Shōwa 56) based on the {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|ja|文化庁}}'s plan for creating "Fudoki no Oka." == Overview == The park covers an extensive area of 19.2 hectares, centered around the {{ill|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|ja|川部・高森古墳群}}, a nationally designated {{ill|historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} that includes six keyhole-shaped burial mounds, notably the oldest such mound in Kyushu, the Akatsuka Kofun, along with surrounding burial mounds. It is situated on a plateau on the right bank of the {{ill|Ekigawa River|ja|駅館川}}, which runs north-south through Usa City. Within the park is the central exhibit and research facility, the '''{{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|ja|大分県立歴史博物館}}'''. This museum was originally established as the Ōita Prefectural Usa Fudoki no Oka Historical Folklore Museum at the same time as the park’s opening. In 1998 (Heisei 10), it underwent expansion to include a permanent exhibition hall and was renamed the Ōita Prefectural Museum of History, which it remains today.https://www.city.usa.oita.jp/tourist/touristspot/touristspot2/touristspot3/10184.html Additionally, various seasonal flowers and trees are planted throughout the park, with cherry blossoms in April and irises in June being particularly famous as scenic highlights. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|ja|大分県立歴史博物館}} * About 5 minutes by car from JR Buzen-Nagasu Station |official_site = [https://www.pref.oita.jp/site/rekishihakubutsukan/ Ōita Prefectural Board of Education Official Page] }} '''Usa Fudoki no Oka''' (宇佐風土記の丘) is an {{ill|open-air museum|ja|野外博物館}} and historic site {{ill|park|ja|公園}} located in Kyōzuka, Takamori, {{ill|Usa, Ōita|ja|宇佐市}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, established in 1981 (Shōwa 56) based on the {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|ja|文化庁}}'s plan for creating "Fudoki no Oka." == Overview == The park covers an extensive area of 19.2 hectares, centered around the {{ill|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|ja|川部・高森古墳群}}, a nationally designated {{ill|historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} that includes six keyhole-shaped burial mounds, notably the oldest such mound in Kyushu, the Akatsuka Kofun, along with surrounding burial mounds. It is situated on a plateau on the right bank of the {{ill|Ekigawa River|ja|駅館川}}, which runs north-south through Usa City. Within the park is the central exhibit and research facility, the '''{{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|ja|大分県立歴史博物館}}'''. This museum was originally established as the Ōita Prefectural Usa Fudoki no Oka Historical Folklore Museum at the same time as the park’s opening. In 1998 (Heisei 10), it underwent expansion to include a permanent exhibition hall and was renamed the Ōita Prefectural Museum of History, which it remains today.https://www.city.usa.oita.jp/tourist/touristspot/touristspot2/touristspot3/10184.html Additionally, various seasonal flowers and trees are planted throughout the park, with cherry blossoms in April and irises in June being particularly famous as scenic highlights. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|ja|大分県立歴史博物館}} * About 5 minutes by car from JR Yanagigaura Station
About 5 minutes by car from JR Buzen-Nagasu Station |official_site = [https://www.pref.oita.jp/site/rekishihakubutsukan/ Ōita Prefectural Board of Education Official Page] }} '''Usa Fudoki no Oka''' (宇佐風土記の丘) is an {{ill|open-air museum|ja|野外博物館}} and historic site {{ill|park|ja|公園}} located in Kyōzuka, Takamori, {{ill|Usa, Ōita|ja|宇佐市}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, established in 1981 (Shōwa 56) based on the {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|ja|文化庁}}'s plan for creating "Fudoki no Oka." == Overview == The park covers an extensive area of 19.2 hectares, centered around the {{ill|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|ja|川部・高森古墳群}}, a nationally designated {{ill|historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} that includes six keyhole-shaped Kofuns, notably the oldest such mound in Kyushu, the Akatsuka Kofun, along with surrounding Kofuns. It is situated on a plateau on the right bank of the {{ill|Ekigawa River|ja|駅館川}}, which runs north-south through Usa City. Within the park is the central exhibit and research facility, the '''{{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|ja|大分県立歴史博物館}}'''. This museum was originally established as the Ōita Prefectural Usa Fudoki no Oka Historical Folklore Museum at the same time as the park’s opening. In 1998 (Heisei 10), it underwent expansion to include a permanent exhibition hall and was renamed the Ōita Prefectural Museum of History, which it remains today.https://www.city.usa.oita.jp/tourist/touristspot/touristspot2/touristspot3/10184.html Additionally, various seasonal flowers and trees are planted throughout the park, with cherry blossoms in April and irises in June being particularly famous as scenic highlights. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|ja|大分県立歴史博物館}} * {{ill|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|ja|川部・高森古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://rekisihakubutukan-b.oita-ed.jp/rekihaku/usafudoki.html Historic Site Park "Usa Fudoki no Oka"] {{Infobox park |name = Usa Fudoki no Oka |english_name = Usa Fudoki no Oka Park |image = Oita Prefectural Museum of History in Usa city, Oita prefecture.JPG |image_caption = Main facility: {{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|ja|大分県立歴史博物館}} |country = Japan |city = {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, {{ill|Usa, Ōita|ja|宇佐市}} |latitude_deg = 33 |latitude_min = 32 |latitude_sec = 32.3 |longitude_deg = 131 |longitude_min = 21 |longitude_sec = 36.9 |map = Japan Oita |type = Historic Site Park |area = 19.2 hectares{{Cite web|ja|author=Usa City Official Tourism Site|url=https://www.city.usa.oita.jp/tourist/touristspot/touristspot2/touristspot3/10184.html|title=Ōita Prefectural Museum of History and Fudoki no Oka|work=Usa City|accessdate=2022-05-02}} |former_name = Ōita Prefectural Usa Fudoki no Oka Historical Folklore Museum |established = 1981 (Shōwa 56) |operator = Ōita Prefecture |parking = 150 cars, 13 large buses |headquarters = {{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|ja|大分県立歴史博物館}} |headquarters_address = Takamori, Usa City, Kyōzuka {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|32|32.3|N|131|21|36.9|E}}}} |access = About 10 minutes by car from JR Usa Station
About 5 minutes by car from JR Yanagigaura Station
About 5 minutes by car from JR Buzen-Nagasu Station |official_site = [https://www.pref.oita.jp/site/rekishihakubutsukan/ Ōita Prefectural Board of Education Official Page] }} '''Usa Fudoki no Oka''' (宇佐風土記の丘) is an {{ill|open-air museum|ja|野外博物館}} and historic site {{ill|park|ja|公園}} located in Kyōzuka, Takamori, {{ill|Usa, Ōita|ja|宇佐市}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, established in 1981 (Shōwa 56) based on the {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|ja|文化庁}}'s plan for creating "Fudoki no Oka." == Overview == The park covers an extensive area of 19.2 hectares, centered around the {{ill|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|ja|川部・高森古墳群}}, a nationally designated {{ill|historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} that includes six keyhole-shaped Kofuns, notably the oldest such mound in Kyushu, the Akatsuka Kofun, along with surrounding Kofuns. It is situated on a plateau on the right bank of the {{ill|Ekigawa River|ja|駅館川}}, which runs north-south through Usa City. Within the park is the central exhibit and research facility, the '''{{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|ja|大分県立歴史博物館}}'''. This museum was originally established as the Ōita Prefectural Usa Fudoki no Oka Historical Folklore Museum at the same time as the park’s opening. In 1998 (Heisei 10), it underwent expansion to include a permanent exhibition hall and was renamed the Ōita Prefectural Museum of History, which it remains today.https://www.city.usa.oita.jp/tourist/touristspot/touristspot2/touristspot3/10184.html Additionally, various seasonal flowers and trees are planted throughout the park, with cherry blossoms in April and irises in June being particularly famous as scenic highlights. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|ja|大分県立歴史博物館}} * {{ill|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|ja|川部・高森古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://rekisihakubutukan-b.oita-ed.jp/rekihaku/usafudoki.html Historic Site Park "Usa Fudoki no Oka"] About 5 minutes by car from JR Yanagigaura Station
About 5 minutes by car from JR Buzen-Nagasu Station |official_site = [https://www.pref.oita.jp/site/rekishihakubutsukan/ Ōita Prefectural Board of Education Official Page] }} '''Usa Fudoki no Oka''' (宇佐風土記の丘) is an {{ill|open-air museum|ja|野外博物館}} and historic site {{ill|park|ja|公園}} located in Kyōzuka, Takamori, {{ill|Usa, Ōita|ja|宇佐市}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, established in 1981 (Shōwa 56) based on the {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|ja|文化庁}}'s plan for creating "Fudoki no Oka." == Overview == The park covers an extensive area of 19.2 hectares, centered around the {{ill|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|ja|川部・高森古墳群}}, a nationally designated {{ill|historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} that includes six keyhole-shaped Kofuns, notably the oldest such mound in Kyushu, the Akatsuka Kofun, along with surrounding Kofuns. It is situated on a plateau on the right bank of the {{ill|Ekigawa River|ja|駅館川}}, which runs north-south through Usa City. Within the park is the central exhibit and research facility, the '''{{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|ja|大分県立歴史博物館}}'''. This museum was originally established as the Ōita Prefectural Usa Fudoki no Oka Historical Folklore Museum at the same time as the park’s opening. In 1998 (Heisei 10), it underwent expansion to include a permanent exhibition hall and was renamed the Ōita Prefectural Museum of History, which it remains today.https://www.city.usa.oita.jp/tourist/touristspot/touristspot2/touristspot3/10184.html Additionally, various seasonal flowers and trees are planted throughout the park, with cherry blossoms in April and irises in June being particularly famous as scenic highlights. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|ja|大分県立歴史博物館}} * {{Infobox park |name = Usa Fudoki no Oka |english_name = Usa Fudoki no Oka Park |image = Oita Prefectural Museum of History in Usa city, Oita prefecture.JPG |image_caption = Main facility: {{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|ja|大分県立歴史博物館}} |country = Japan |city = {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, {{ill|Usa, Ōita|ja|宇佐市}} |latitude_deg = 33 |latitude_min = 32 |latitude_sec = 32.3 |longitude_deg = 131 |longitude_min = 21 |longitude_sec = 36.9 |map = Japan Oita |type = Historic Site Park |area = 19.2 hectares{{Cite web|ja|author=Usa City Official Tourism Site|url=https://www.city.usa.oita.jp/tourist/touristspot/touristspot2/touristspot3/10184.html|title=Ōita Prefectural Museum of History and Fudoki no Oka|work=Usa City|accessdate=2022-05-02}} |former_name = Ōita Prefectural Usa Fudoki no Oka Historical Folklore Museum |established = 1981 (Shōwa 56) |operator = Ōita Prefecture |parking = 150 cars, 13 large buses |headquarters = {{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|ja|大分県立歴史博物館}} |headquarters_address = Takamori, Usa City, Kyōzuka {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|32|32.3|N|131|21|36.9|E}}}} |access = About 10 minutes by car from JR Usa Station
About 5 minutes by car from JR Yanagigaura Station
About 5 minutes by car from JR Buzen-Nagasu Station |official_site = [https://www.pref.oita.jp/site/rekishihakubutsukan/ Ōita Prefectural Board of Education Official Page] }} '''Usa Fudoki no Oka''' (宇佐風土記の丘) is an {{ill|open-air museum|ja|野外博物館}} and historic site {{ill|park|ja|公園}} located in Kyōzuka, Takamori, {{ill|Usa, Ōita|ja|宇佐市}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, established in 1981 (Shōwa 56) based on the {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|ja|文化庁}}'s plan for creating "Fudoki no Oka." == Overview == The park covers an extensive area of 19.2 hectares, centered around the {{ill|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|ja|川部・高森古墳群}}, a nationally designated {{ill|historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} that includes six keyhole-shaped Kofuns, notably the oldest such mound in Kyushu, the Akatsuka Kofun, along with surrounding Kofuns. It is situated on a plateau on the right bank of the {{ill|Ekigawa River|ja|駅館川}}, which runs north-south through Usa City. Within the park is the central exhibit and research facility, the '''{{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|ja|大分県立歴史博物館}}'''. This museum was originally established as the Ōita Prefectural Usa Fudoki no Oka Historical Folklore Museum at the same time as the park’s opening. In 1998 (Heisei 10), it underwent expansion to include a permanent exhibition hall and was renamed the Ōita Prefectural Museum of History, which it remains today.https://www.city.usa.oita.jp/tourist/touristspot/touristspot2/touristspot3/10184.html Additionally, various seasonal flowers and trees are planted throughout the park, with cherry blossoms in April and irises in June being particularly famous as scenic highlights. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|ja|大分県立歴史博物館}} * {{ill|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|ja|川部・高森古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://rekisihakubutukan-b.oita-ed.jp/rekihaku/usafudoki.html Historic Site Park "Usa Fudoki no Oka"] About 5 minutes by car from JR Yanagigaura Station
About 5 minutes by car from JR Buzen-Nagasu Station |official_site = [https://www.pref.oita.jp/site/rekishihakubutsukan/ Ōita Prefectural Board of Education Official Page] }} '''Usa Fudoki no Oka''' (宇佐風土記の丘) is an {{ill|open-air museum|ja|野外博物館|12=draft}} and historic site {{ill|park|ja|公園|12=draft}} located in Kyōzuka, Takamori, {{ill|Usa, Ōita|ja|宇佐市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県|12=draft}}, established in 1981 (Shōwa 56) based on the {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|ja|文化庁|12=draft}}'s plan for creating "Fudoki no Oka." == Overview == The park covers an extensive area of 19.2 hectares, centered around the {{ill|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|ja|川部・高森古墳群|12=draft}}, a nationally designated {{ill|historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}} that includes six keyhole-shaped Kofuns, notably the oldest such mound in Kyushu, the Akatsuka Kofun, along with surrounding Kofuns. It is situated on a plateau on the right bank of the {{ill|Ekigawa River|ja|駅館川|12=draft}}, which runs north-south through Usa City. Within the park is the central exhibit and research facility, the '''{{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|ja|大分県立歴史博物館|12=draft}}'''. This museum was originally established as the Ōita Prefectural Usa Fudoki no Oka Historical Folklore Museum at the same time as the park’s opening. In 1998 (Heisei 10), it underwent expansion to include a permanent exhibition hall and was renamed the Ōita Prefectural Museum of History, which it remains today.https://www.city.usa.oita.jp/tourist/touristspot/touristspot2/touristspot3/10184.html Additionally, various seasonal flowers and trees are planted throughout the park, with cherry blossoms in April and irises in June being particularly famous as scenic highlights. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|ja|大分県立歴史博物館|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|ja|川部・高森古墳群|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://rekisihakubutukan-b.oita-ed.jp/rekihaku/usafudoki.html Historic Site Park "Usa Fudoki no Oka"] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Usa Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Usa Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Usa Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Parks in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Usa, Ōita]] [[Category:History of Usa, Ōita]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Usa Fudoki Hill]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fudoki Hills]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:宇佐風土記の丘]] [[ja:%E5%AE%87%E4%BD%90%E9%A2%A8%E5%9C%9F%E8%A8%98%E3%81%AE%E4%B8%98]] [[sn:Usa Fudoki Hill]] [[en:Usa Fudoki Hill]] [[sn:sa Fudoki Hill]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Usa Fudoki Hill}}

About 5 minutes by car from JR Buzen-Nagasu Station |official_site = [https://www.pref.oita.jp/site/rekishihakubutsukan/ Ōita Prefectural Board of Education Official Page] }} '''Usa Fudoki no Oka''' (宇佐風土記の丘) is an {{ill|open-air museum|ja|野外博物館|12=draft}} and historic site {{ill|park|ja|公園|12=draft}} located in Kyōzuka, Takamori, {{ill|Usa, Ōita|ja|宇佐市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県|12=draft}}, established in 1981 (Shōwa 56) based on the {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|ja|文化庁|12=draft}}'s plan for creating "Fudoki no Oka." == Overview == The park covers an extensive area of 19.2 hectares, centered around the {{ill|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|ja|川部・高森古墳群|12=draft}}, a nationally designated {{ill|historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}} that includes six keyhole-shaped Kofuns, notably the oldest such mound in Kyushu, the Akatsuka Kofun, along with surrounding Kofuns. It is situated on a plateau on the right bank of the {{ill|Ekigawa River|ja|駅館川|12=draft}}, which runs north-south through Usa City. Within the park is the central exhibit and research facility, the '''{{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|ja|大分県立歴史博物館|12=draft}}'''. This museum was originally established as the Ōita Prefectural Usa Fudoki no Oka Historical Folklore Museum at the same time as the park’s opening. In 1998 (Heisei 10), it underwent expansion to include a permanent exhibition hall and was renamed the Ōita Prefectural Museum of History, which it remains today.https://www.city.usa.oita.jp/tourist/touristspot/touristspot2/touristspot3/10184.html Additionally, various seasonal flowers and trees are planted throughout the park, with cherry blossoms in April and irises in June being particularly famous as scenic highlights. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|ja|大分県立歴史博物館|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|ja|川部・高森古墳群|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://rekisihakubutukan-b.oita-ed.jp/rekihaku/usafudoki.html Historic Site Park "Usa Fudoki no Oka"] {{Infobox park |name = Usa Fudoki no Oka |english_name = Usa Fudoki no Oka Park |image = Oita Prefectural Museum of History in Usa city, Oita prefecture.JPG |image_caption = Main facility: {{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|ja|大分県立歴史博物館|12=draft}} |country = Japan |city = {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Usa, Ōita|ja|宇佐市|12=draft}} |latitude_deg = 33 |latitude_min = 32 |latitude_sec = 32.3 |longitude_deg = 131 |longitude_min = 21 |longitude_sec = 36.9 |map = Japan Oita |type = Historic Site Park |area = 19.2 hectares{{Cite web|ja|author=Usa City Official Tourism Site|url=https://www.city.usa.oita.jp/tourist/touristspot/touristspot2/touristspot3/10184.html|title=Ōita Prefectural Museum of History and Fudoki no Oka|work=Usa City|accessdate=2022-05-02}} |former_name = Ōita Prefectural Usa Fudoki no Oka Historical Folklore Museum |established = 1981 (Shōwa 56) |operator = Ōita Prefecture |parking = 150 cars, 13 large buses |headquarters = {{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|ja|大分県立歴史博物館|12=draft}} |headquarters_address = Takamori, Usa City, Kyōzuka {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|32|32.3|N|131|21|36.9|E}}}} |access = About 10 minutes by car from JR Usa Station
About 5 minutes by car from JR Yanagigaura Station
About 5 minutes by car from JR Buzen-Nagasu Station |official_site = [https://www.pref.oita.jp/site/rekishihakubutsukan/ Ōita Prefectural Board of Education Official Page] }} '''Usa Fudoki no Oka''' (宇佐風土記の丘) is an {{ill|open-air museum|ja|野外博物館|12=draft}} and historic site {{ill|park|ja|公園|12=draft}} located in Kyōzuka, Takamori, {{ill|Usa, Ōita|ja|宇佐市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県|12=draft}}, established in 1981 (Shōwa 56) based on the {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|ja|文化庁|12=draft}}'s plan for creating "Fudoki no Oka." == Overview == The park covers an extensive area of 19.2 hectares, centered around the {{ill|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|ja|川部・高森古墳群|12=draft}}, a nationally designated {{ill|historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}} that includes six keyhole-shaped Kofuns, notably the oldest such mound in Kyushu, the Akatsuka Kofun, along with surrounding Kofuns. It is situated on a plateau on the right bank of the {{ill|Ekigawa River|ja|駅館川|12=draft}}, which runs north-south through Usa City. Within the park is the central exhibit and research facility, the '''{{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|ja|大分県立歴史博物館|12=draft}}'''. This museum was originally established as the Ōita Prefectural Usa Fudoki no Oka Historical Folklore Museum at the same time as the park’s opening. In 1998 (Heisei 10), it underwent expansion to include a permanent exhibition hall and was renamed the Ōita Prefectural Museum of History, which it remains today.https://www.city.usa.oita.jp/tourist/touristspot/touristspot2/touristspot3/10184.html Additionally, various seasonal flowers and trees are planted throughout the park, with cherry blossoms in April and irises in June being particularly famous as scenic highlights. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|ja|大分県立歴史博物館|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|ja|川部・高森古墳群|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://rekisihakubutukan-b.oita-ed.jp/rekihaku/usafudoki.html Historic Site Park "Usa Fudoki no Oka"] {{Infobox park |name = Usa Fudoki no Oka |english_name = Usa Fudoki no Oka Park |image = Oita Prefectural Museum of History in Usa city, Oita prefecture.JPG |image_caption = Main facility: {{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|ja|大分県立歴史博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} |country = Japan |city = {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Usa, Ōita|ja|宇佐市|12=draft|12=draft}} |latitude_deg = 33 |latitude_min = 32 |latitude_sec = 32.3 |longitude_deg = 131 |longitude_min = 21 |longitude_sec = 36.9 |map = Japan Oita |type = Historic Site Park |area = 19.2 hectares{{Cite web|ja|author=Usa City Official Tourism Site|url=https://www.city.usa.oita.jp/tourist/touristspot/touristspot2/touristspot3/10184.html|title=Ōita Prefectural Museum of History and Fudoki no Oka|work=Usa City|accessdate=2022-05-02}} |former_name = Ōita Prefectural Usa Fudoki no Oka Historical Folklore Museum |established = 1981 (Shōwa 56) |operator = Ōita Prefecture |parking = 150 cars, 13 large buses |headquarters = {{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|ja|大分県立歴史博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} |headquarters_address = Takamori, Usa City, Kyōzuka {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|32|32.3|N|131|21|36.9|E}}}} |access = About 10 minutes by car from JR Usa Station
About 5 minutes by car from JR Yanagigaura Station
About 5 minutes by car from JR Buzen-Nagasu Station |official_site = [https://www.pref.oita.jp/site/rekishihakubutsukan/ Ōita Prefectural Board of Education Official Page] }} '''Usa Fudoki no Oka''' (宇佐風土記の丘) is an {{ill|open-air museum|ja|野外博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} and historic site {{ill|park|ja|公園|12=draft|12=draft}} located in Kyōzuka, Takamori, {{ill|Usa, Ōita|ja|宇佐市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県|12=draft|12=draft}}, established in 1981 (Shōwa 56) based on the {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|ja|文化庁|12=draft|12=draft}}'s plan for creating "Fudoki no Oka." == Overview == The park covers an extensive area of 19.2 hectares, centered around the {{ill|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|ja|川部・高森古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}, a nationally designated {{ill|historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft|12=draft}}} that includes six keyhole-shaped Kofuns, notably the oldest such mound in Kyushu, the Akatsuka Kofun, along with surrounding Kofuns. It is situated on a plateau on the right bank of the {{ill|Ekigawa River|ja|駅館川|12=draft|12=draft}}, which runs north-south through Usa City. Within the park is the central exhibit and research facility, the '''{{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|ja|大分県立歴史博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}'''. This museum was originally established as the Ōita Prefectural Usa Fudoki no Oka Historical Folklore Museum at the same time as the park’s opening. In 1998 (Heisei 10), it underwent expansion to include a permanent exhibition hall and was renamed the Ōita Prefectural Museum of History, which it remains today.https://www.city.usa.oita.jp/tourist/touristspot/touristspot2/touristspot3/10184.html Additionally, various seasonal flowers and trees are planted throughout the park, with cherry blossoms in April and irises in June being particularly famous as scenic highlights. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|ja|大分県立歴史博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|ja|川部・高森古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://rekisihakubutukan-b.oita-ed.jp/rekihaku/usafudoki.html Historic Site Park "Usa Fudoki no Oka"] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Usa Fudoki Hill}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Parks in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Usa, Ōita]] [[Category:History of Usa, Ōita]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fudoki Hills]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Usa Fudoki Hill]] [[ja:宇佐風土記の丘]] [[ja:%E5%AE%87%E4%BD%90%E9%A2%A8%E5%9C%9F%E8%A8%98%E3%81%AE%E4%B8%98]] [[en:Usa Fudoki Hill]] [[sn:sa Fudoki Hill]]

It is situated on a plateau on the right bank of the {{ill|Ekigawa River|ja|駅館川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which runs north-south through Usa City. Within the park is the central exhibit and research facility, the '''{{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|ja|大分県立歴史博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'''. This museum was originally established as the Ōita Prefectural Usa Fudoki no Oka Historical Folklore Museum at the same time as the park’s opening. In 1998 (Heisei 10), it underwent expansion to include a permanent exhibition hall and was renamed the Ōita Prefectural Museum of History, which it remains today.https://www.city.usa.oita.jp/tourist/touristspot/touristspot2/touristspot3/10184.html Additionally, various seasonal flowers and trees are planted throughout the park, with cherry blossoms in April and irises in June being particularly famous as scenic highlights. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|ja|大分県立歴史博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|ja|川部・高森古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://rekisihakubutukan-b.oita-ed.jp/rekihaku/usafudoki.html Historic Site Park "Usa Fudoki no Oka"] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Usa Fudoki Hill}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Parks in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Usa, Ōita]] [[Category:History of Usa, Ōita]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fudoki Hills]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Usa Fudoki Hill]] [[ja:宇佐風土記の丘]] [[ja:%E5%AE%87%E4%BD%90%E9%A2%A8%E5%9C%9F%E8%A8%98%E3%81%AE%E4%B8%98]] [[en:Usa Fudoki Hill]] [[sn:sa Fudoki Hill]]

revision.comment: 8 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:宇佐風土記の丘 up to rev 97995589
revision.text:
{{公園 |名称 = 宇佐風土記の丘 |英語名称 =Usa Fudoki no Oka Park |画像 =Oita Prefectural Museum of History in Usa city, Oita prefecture.JPG |画像キャプション = 主要施設の[[大分県立歴史博物館]] |国 = 日本 |都市 =[[大分県]][[宇佐市]] | 緯度度 =33|緯度分 =32|緯度秒 =32.3 | 経度度 =131|経度分 =21|経度秒 =36.9 |map=Japan Oita |分類 = 史跡公園 |面積 = 19.2ヘクタール{{Cite web|和書|author=宇佐市公式観光サイト|url=https://www.city.usa.oita.jp/tourist/touristspot/touristspot2/touristspot3/10184.html|title=大分県立歴史博物館・風土記の丘|work=宇佐市|accessdate=2022-05-02}} |前身 = 大分県立宇佐風土記の丘歴史民俗資料館 |開園 = [[1981年]]（昭和56年） |運営者 = 大分県 |駐車場 = 乗用車150台、大型バス13台 |事務所 = [[大分県立歴史博物館]] |事務所所在地 =宇佐市大字高森字京塚 {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|32|32.3|N|131|21|36.9|E}}}} |アクセス =JR宇佐駅から車で約10分
JR柳ヶ浦駅から車で約5分
JR豊前長洲駅から車で約5分 |公式サイト = [https://www.pref.oita.jp/site/rekishihakubutsukan/ 大分県教育委員会公式ページ] }} '''宇佐風土記の丘'''（うさふどきのおか）は、[[文化庁]]の[[風土記の丘]]設置構想に基づき、[[1981年]]（昭和56年）に[[大分県]][[宇佐市]]大字高森字京塚に開設された[[野外博物館]]・史跡[[公園]]である。 == 概要 == 九州最古の[[前方後円墳]]である赤塚古墳をはじめとするの6基の前方後円墳及びその周囲の古墳群からなり、国の[[史跡]]に指定されている[[川部・高森古墳群]]を中心とした総面積19.2ヘクタールの大規模な史跡公園で、宇佐市を南から北に縦貫する[[駅館川]]右岸の台地上に位置している。 敷地内には、その中心となる展示・研究施設として、'''[[大分県立歴史博物館]]'''が開設されている。この博物館は、宇佐風土記の丘の開設と同時に大分県立宇佐風土記の丘歴史民俗資料館として開館し、1998年（平成10年）に常設展示館の増設等を行なうとともに大分県立歴史博物館に改称して、現在に至っている。 また、園内各所には、四季の花木が植栽されており、特に、4月の桜と6月の菖蒲は、名所として有名である。 ==脚注== {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[大分県立歴史博物館]] * [[川部・高森古墳群]] == 外部リンク == * [http://rekisihakubutukan-b.oita-ed.jp/rekihaku/usafudoki.html 史跡公園「宇佐風土記の丘」] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うさふときのおか}} [[Category:大分県の公園]] [[Category:大分県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:宇佐市の地理]] [[Category:宇佐市の歴史]]

About 5 minutes by car from JR Buzen-Nagasu Station |official_site = [https://www.pref.oita.jp/site/rekishihakubutsukan/ Ōita Prefectural Board of Education Official Page] }} '''Usa Fudoki no Oka''' (宇佐風土記の丘) is an {{ill|open-air museum|ja|野外博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and historic site {{ill|park|ja|公園|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in Kyōzuka, Takamori, {{ill|Usa, Ōita|ja|宇佐市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, established in 1981 (Shōwa 56) based on the {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|ja|文化庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s plan for creating "Fudoki no Oka." == Overview == The park covers an extensive area of 19.2 hectares, centered around the {{ill|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|ja|川部・高森古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a nationally designated {{ill|historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|qq=draft|qq=draft}}} that includes six keyhole-shaped Kofuns, notably the oldest such mound in Kyushu, the Akatsuka Kofun, along with surrounding Kofuns. It is situated on a plateau on the right bank of the {{ill|Ekigawa River|ja|駅館川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which runs north-south through Usa City. Within the park is the central exhibit and research facility, the '''{{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|ja|大分県立歴史博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'''. This museum was originally established as the Ōita Prefectural Usa Fudoki no Oka Historical Folklore Museum at the same time as the park’s opening. In 1998 (Heisei 10), it underwent expansion to include a permanent exhibition hall and was renamed the Ōita Prefectural Museum of History, which it remains today.https://www.city.usa.oita.jp/tourist/touristspot/touristspot2/touristspot3/10184.html Additionally, various seasonal flowers and trees are planted throughout the park, with cherry blossoms in April and irises in June being particularly famous as scenic highlights. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|ja|大分県立歴史博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|ja|川部・高森古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://rekisihakubutukan-b.oita-ed.jp/rekihaku/usafudoki.html Historic Site Park "Usa Fudoki no Oka"] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{citation needed|Usa Fudoki Hill}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|宇佐風土記の丘|version=97995589|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Parks in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Usa, Ōita]] [[Category:History of Usa, Ōita]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fudoki Hills]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Usa Fudoki Hill]] [[ja:宇佐風土記の丘]] [[ja:%E5%AE%87%E4%BD%90%E9%A2%A8%E5%9C%9F%E8%A8%98%E3%81%AE%E4%B8%98]] [[en:Usa Fudoki Hill]] [[sn:sa Fudoki Hill]]

{{Infobox park |name = Usa Fudoki no Oka |english_name = Usa Fudoki no Oka Park |image = Oita Prefectural Museum of History in Usa city, Oita prefecture.JPG |image_caption = Main facility: [[:en:Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History]] |country = Japan |city = [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]], [[:en:Usa, Ōita|Usa, Ōita]] |latitude_deg = 33 |latitude_min = 32 |latitude_sec = 32.3 |longitude_deg = 131 |longitude_min = 21 |longitude_sec = 36.9 |map = Japan Oita |type = Historic Site Park |area = 19.2 hectares{{Cite web|ja|author=Usa City Official Tourism Site|url=https://www.city.usa.oita.jp/tourist/touristspot/touristspot2/touristspot3/10184.html|title=Ōita Prefectural Museum of History and Fudoki no Oka|work=Usa City|accessdate=2022-05-02}} |former_name = Ōita Prefectural Usa Fudoki no Oka Historical Folklore Museum |established = 1981 (Shōwa 56) |operator = Ōita Prefecture |parking = 150 cars, 13 large buses |headquarters = [[:en:Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History]] |headquarters_address = Takamori, Usa City, Kyōzuka {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|32|32.3|N|131|21|36.9|E}}}} |access = About 10 minutes by car from JR Usa Station
About 5 minutes by car from JR Yanagigaura Station
About 5 minutes by car from JR Buzen-Nagasu Station |official_site = [https://www.pref.oita.jp/site/rekishihakubutsukan/ Ōita Prefectural Board of Education Official Page] }} '''Usa Fudoki no Oka''' (宇佐風土記の丘) is an [[:en:Open-air museum|open-air museum]] and historic site [[:en:Park|park]] located in Kyōzuka, Takamori, [[:en:Usa, Ōita|Usa, Ōita]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]], established in 1981 (Shōwa 56) based on the [[:en:Agency for Cultural Affairs|Agency for Cultural Affairs]]'s plan for creating "Fudoki no Oka." == Overview == The park covers an extensive area of 19.2 hectares, centered around the [[:en:Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster]], a nationally designated [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} that includes six keyhole-shaped Kofuns, notably the oldest such mound in Kyushu, the Akatsuka Kofun, along with surrounding Kofuns. It is situated on a plateau on the right bank of the {{ill|Ekigawa River|ja|駅館川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekigawa River|qq=}}, which runs north-south through Usa City. Within the park is the central exhibit and research facility, the '''[[:en:Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History]]'''. This museum was originally established as the Ōita Prefectural Usa Fudoki no Oka Historical Folklore Museum at the same time as the park’s opening. In 1998 (Heisei 10), it underwent expansion to include a permanent exhibition hall and was renamed the Ōita Prefectural Museum of History, which it remains today.https://www.city.usa.oita.jp/tourist/touristspot/touristspot2/touristspot3/10184.html Additionally, various seasonal flowers and trees are planted throughout the park, with cherry blossoms in April and irises in June being particularly famous as scenic highlights. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History]] * [[:en:Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster]] == External Links == * [http://rekisihakubutukan-b.oita-ed.jp/rekihaku/usafudoki.html Historic Site Park "Usa Fudoki no Oka"] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|宇佐風土記の丘|version=97995589|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Parks in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Usa, Ōita]] [[Category:History of Usa, Ōita]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fudoki Hills]] }} [[sn:Usa Fudoki Hill]] [[ja:宇佐風土記の丘]] [[ja:%E5%AE%87%E4%BD%90%E9%A2%A8%E5%9C%9F%E8%A8%98%E3%81%AE%E4%B8%98]] [[en:Usa Fudoki Hill]] [[sn:sa Fudoki Hill]]

{{Infobox park |name = Usa Fudoki no Oka |english_name = Usa Fudoki no Oka Park |image = Oita Prefectural Museum of History in Usa city, Oita prefecture.JPG |image_caption = Main facility: [[:en:Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History]] |country = Japan |city = [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]], [[:en:Usa, Ōita|Usa, Ōita]] |latitude_deg = 33 |latitude_min = 32 |latitude_sec = 32.3 |longitude_deg = 131 |longitude_min = 21 |longitude_sec = 36.9 |map = Japan Oita |type = Historic Site Park |area = 19.2 hectares{{Cite web|ja|author=Usa City Official Tourism Site|url=https://www.city.usa.oita.jp/tourist/touristspot/touristspot2/touristspot3/10184.html|title=Ōita Prefectural Museum of History and Fudoki no Oka|work=Usa City|accessdate=2022-05-02}} |former_name = Ōita Prefectural Usa Fudoki no Oka Historical Folklore Museum |established = 1981 (Shōwa 56) |operator = Ōita Prefecture |parking = 150 cars, 13 large buses |headquarters = [[:en:Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History]] |headquarters_address = Takamori, Usa City, Kyōzuka {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|32|32.3|N|131|21|36.9|E}}}} |access = About 10 minutes by car from JR Usa Station
About 5 minutes by car from JR Yanagigaura Station
About 5 minutes by car from JR Buzen-Nagasu Station |official_site = [https://www.pref.oita.jp/site/rekishihakubutsukan/ Ōita Prefectural Board of Education Official Page] }} '''Usa Fudoki no Oka''' (宇佐風土記の丘) is an [[:en:Open-air museum|open-air museum]] and historic site [[:en:Park|park]] located in Kyōzuka, Takamori, [[:en:Usa, Ōita|Usa, Ōita]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]], established in 1981 (Shōwa 56) based on the [[:en:Agency for Cultural Affairs|Agency for Cultural Affairs]]'s plan for creating "Fudoki no Oka." == Overview == The park covers an extensive area of 19.2 hectares, centered around the [[:en:Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster]], a nationally designated [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} that includes six keyhole-shaped Kofuns, notably the oldest such mound in Kyushu, the Akatsuka Kofun, along with surrounding Kofuns. It is situated on a plateau on the right bank of the {{ill|Ekigawa River|ja|駅館川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekigawa River|qq=}}, which runs north-south through Usa City. Within the park is the central exhibit and research facility, the '''[[:en:Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History]]'''. This museum was originally established as the Ōita Prefectural Usa Fudoki no Oka Historical Folklore Museum at the same time as the park’s opening. In 1998 (Heisei 10), it underwent expansion to include a permanent exhibition hall and was renamed the Ōita Prefectural Museum of History, which it remains today.https://www.city.usa.oita.jp/tourist/touristspot/touristspot2/touristspot3/10184.html Additionally, various seasonal flowers and trees are planted throughout the park, with cherry blossoms in April and irises in June being particularly famous as scenic highlights. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History]] * [[:en:Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster]] == External Links == * [http://rekisihakubutukan-b.oita-ed.jp/rekihaku/usafudoki.html Historic Site Park "Usa Fudoki no Oka"] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|宇佐風土記の丘|version=97995589|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Parks in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Usa, Ōita]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Usa Fudoki Hill]] [[ja:宇佐風土記の丘]] [[ja:%E5%AE%87%E4%BD%90%E9%A2%A8%E5%9C%9F%E8%A8%98%E3%81%AE%E4%B8%98]] [[sn:sa Fudoki Hill]] [[Category:National historic sites in Oita prefecture]] [[Category:Parks and gardens in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Usa, Oita]] [[Category:宇佐市の歴史]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11449110]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox park |name = Usa Fudoki no Oka |english_name = Usa Fudoki no Oka Park |image = Oita Prefectural Museum of History in Usa city, Oita prefecture.JPG |image_caption = Main facility: [[:en:Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History]] |country = Japan |city = [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]], [[:en:Usa, Ōita|Usa, Ōita]] |latitude_deg = 33 |latitude_min = 32 |latitude_sec = 32.3 |longitude_deg = 131 |longitude_min = 21 |longitude_sec = 36.9 |map = Japan Oita |type = Historic Site Park |area = 19.2 hectares{{Cite web|ja|author=Usa City Official Tourism Site|url=https://www.city.usa.oita.jp/tourist/touristspot/touristspot2/touristspot3/10184.html|title=Ōita Prefectural Museum of History and Fudoki no Oka|work=Usa City|accessdate=2022-05-02}} |former_name = Ōita Prefectural Usa Fudoki no Oka Historical Folklore Museum |established = 1981 (Shōwa 56) |operator = Ōita Prefecture |parking = 150 cars, 13 large buses |headquarters = [[:en:Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History]] |headquarters_address = Takamori, Usa City, Kyōzuka {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|32|32.3|N|131|21|36.9|E}}}} |access = About 10 minutes by car from JR Usa Station
About 5 minutes by car from JR Yanagigaura Station
About 5 minutes by car from JR Buzen-Nagasu Station |official_site = [https://www.pref.oita.jp/site/rekishihakubutsukan/ Ōita Prefectural Board of Education Official Page] }} '''Usa Fudoki no Oka''' (宇佐風土記の丘) is an [[:en:Open-air museum|open-air museum]] and historic site [[:en:Park|park]] located in Kyōzuka, Takamori, [[:en:Usa, Ōita|Usa, Ōita]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]], established in 1981 (Shōwa 56) based on the [[:en:Agency for Cultural Affairs|Agency for Cultural Affairs]]'s plan for creating "Fudoki no Oka." == Overview == The park covers an extensive area of 19.2 hectares, centered around the [[:en:Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster]], a nationally designated [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} that includes six keyhole-shaped Kofuns, notably the oldest such mound in Kyushu, the Akatsuka Kofun, along with surrounding Kofuns. It is situated on a plateau on the right bank of the {{ill|Ekigawa River|ja|駅館川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekigawa River|qq=|1=Yakkan River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668226}}, which runs north-south through Usa City. Within the park is the central exhibit and research facility, the '''[[:en:Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History]]'''. This museum was originally established as the Ōita Prefectural Usa Fudoki no Oka Historical Folklore Museum at the same time as the park’s opening. In 1998 (Heisei 10), it underwent expansion to include a permanent exhibition hall and was renamed the Ōita Prefectural Museum of History, which it remains today.https://www.city.usa.oita.jp/tourist/touristspot/touristspot2/touristspot3/10184.html Additionally, various seasonal flowers and trees are planted throughout the park, with cherry blossoms in April and irises in June being particularly famous as scenic highlights. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History]] * [[:en:Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster]] == External Links == * [http://rekisihakubutukan-b.oita-ed.jp/rekihaku/usafudoki.html Historic Site Park "Usa Fudoki no Oka"] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|宇佐風土記の丘|version=97995589|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Usa Fudoki Hill]] [[ja:宇佐風土記の丘]] [[ja:%E5%AE%87%E4%BD%90%E9%A2%A8%E5%9C%9F%E8%A8%98%E3%81%AE%E4%B8%98]] [[sn:sa Fudoki Hill]] [[Category:National historic sites in Oita prefecture]] [[Category:Parks and gardens in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Usa, Oita]] [[Category:History of Usa City]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Usa Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]]

Within the park is the central exhibit and research facility, the '''[[:en:Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History]]'''. This museum was originally established as the Ōita Prefectural Usa Fudoki no Oka Historical Folklore Museum at the same time as the park’s opening. In 1998 (Heisei 10), it underwent expansion to include a permanent exhibition hall and was renamed the Ōita Prefectural Museum of History, which it remains today.https://www.city.usa.oita.jp/tourist/touristspot/touristspot2/touristspot3/10184.html Additionally, various seasonal flowers and trees are planted throughout the park, with cherry blossoms in April and irises in June being particularly famous as scenic highlights. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History]] * [[:en:Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster]] == External Links == * [http://rekisihakubutukan-b.oita-ed.jp/rekihaku/usafudoki.html Historic Site Park "Usa Fudoki no Oka"] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|宇佐風土記の丘|version=97995589|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Usa Fudoki Hill]] [[ja:宇佐風土記の丘]] [[ja:%E5%AE%87%E4%BD%90%E9%A2%A8%E5%9C%9F%E8%A8%98%E3%81%AE%E4%B8%98]] [[sn:sa Fudoki Hill]] [[Category:National historic sites in Oita prefecture]] [[Category:Parks and gardens in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Usa, Oita]] [[Category:History of Usa City]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Usa Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]]

About 5 minutes by car from JR Buzen-Nagasu Station |official_site = [https://www.pref.oita.jp/site/rekishihakubutsukan/ Ōita Prefectural Board of Education Official Page] }} '''Usa Fudoki no Oka''' (宇佐風土記の丘) is an [[:en:Open-air museum|open-air museum]] and historic site [[:en:Park|park]] located in Kyōzuka, Takamori, [[:en:Usa, Ōita|Usa, Ōita]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]], established in 1981 (Shōwa 56) based on the [[:en:Agency for Cultural Affairs|Agency for Cultural Affairs]]'s plan for creating "Fudoki no Oka." == Overview == The park covers an extensive area of 19.2 hectares, centered around the [[:en:Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster]], a nationally designated [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} that includes six keyhole-shaped Kofuns, notably the oldest such mound in Kyushu, the Akatsuka Kofun, along with surrounding Kofuns. It is situated on a plateau on the right bank of the {{ill|Ekigawa River|ja|駅館川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekigawa River|qq=|1=Yakkan River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668226}}, which runs north-south through Usa City. Within the park is the central exhibit and research facility, the '''[[:en:Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History]]'''. This museum was originally established as the Ōita Prefectural Usa Fudoki no Oka Historical Folklore Museum at the same time as the park’s opening. In 1998 (Heisei 10), it underwent expansion to include a permanent exhibition hall and was renamed the Ōita Prefectural Museum of History, which it remains today.https://www.city.usa.oita.jp/tourist/touristspot/touristspot2/touristspot3/10184.html Additionally, various seasonal flowers and trees are planted throughout the park, with cherry blossoms in April and irises in June being particularly famous as scenic highlights. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History]] * [[:en:Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster]] == External Links == * [http://rekisihakubutukan-b.oita-ed.jp/rekihaku/usafudoki.html Historic Site Park "Usa Fudoki no Oka"] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|宇佐風土記の丘|version=97995589|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:宇佐風土記の丘]] [[ja:%E5%AE%87%E4%BD%90%E9%A2%A8%E5%9C%9F%E8%A8%98%E3%81%AE%E4%B8%98]] [[Category:National historic sites in Oita prefecture]] [[Category:Parks and gardens in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Usa, Oita]] [[Category:History of Usa City]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] {{wikidata link|Q11449110}}
