In 1998 (Heisei 10), it underwent expansion to include a permanent exhibition hall and was renamed the Ōita Prefectural Museum of History, which it remains today.https://www.city.usa.oita.jp/tourist/touristspot/touristspot2/touristspot3/10184.html Additionally, various seasonal flowers and trees are planted throughout the park, with cherry blossoms in April and irises in June being particularly famous as scenic highlights. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History]] * [[:en:Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster]] == External Links == * [http://rekisihakubutukan-b.oita-ed.jp/rekihaku/usafudoki.html Historic Site Park "Usa Fudoki no Oka"] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|宇佐風土記の丘|version=97995589|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:宇佐風土記の丘]] [[ja:%E5%AE%87%E4%BD%90%E9%A2%A8%E5%9C%9F%E8%A8%98%E3%81%AE%E4%B8%98]] [[Category:National historic sites in Oita prefecture]] [[Category:Parks and gardens in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Usa, Oita]] [[Category:History of Usa City]] {{wikidata link|Q11449110}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q11449110}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]]

[[ja:宇佐風土記の丘]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11449110]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox park |name = Usa Fudoki no Oka |english_name = Usa Fudoki no Oka Park |image = Oita Prefectural Museum of History in Usa city, Oita prefecture.JPG |image_caption = Main facility: [[:en:Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History]] |country = Japan |city = [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]], [[:en:Usa, Ōita|Usa, Ōita]] |latitude_deg = 33 |latitude_min = 32 |latitude_sec = 32.3 |longitude_deg = 131 |longitude_min = 21 |longitude_sec = 36.9 |map = Japan Oita |type = Historic Site Park |area = 19.2 hectares{{Cite web|ja|author=Usa City Official Tourism Site|url=https://www.city.usa.oita.jp/tourist/touristspot/touristspot2/touristspot3/10184.html|title=Ōita Prefectural Museum of History and Fudoki no Oka|work=Usa City|accessdate=2022-05-02}} |former_name = Ōita Prefectural Usa Fudoki no Oka Historical Folklore Museum |established = 1981 (Shōwa 56) |operator = Ōita Prefecture |parking = 150 cars, 13 large buses |headquarters = [[:en:Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History]] |headquarters_address = Takamori, Usa City, Kyōzuka {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|32|32.3|N|131|21|36.9|E}}}} |access = About 10 minutes by car from JR Usa Station
About 5 minutes by car from JR Yanagigaura Station
About 5 minutes by car from JR Buzen-Nagasu Station |official_site = [https://www.pref.oita.jp/site/rekishihakubutsukan/ Ōita Prefectural Board of Education Official Page] }} '''Usa Fudoki no Oka''' (宇佐風土記の丘) is an [[:en:Open-air museum|open-air museum]] and historic site [[:en:Park|park]] located in Kyōzuka, Takamori, [[:en:Usa, Ōita|Usa, Ōita]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]], established in 1981 (Shōwa 56) based on the [[:en:Agency for Cultural Affairs|Agency for Cultural Affairs]]'s plan for creating "Fudoki no Oka." == Overview == The park covers an extensive area of 19.2 hectares, centered around the [[:en:Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster]], a nationally designated [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} that includes six keyhole-shaped Kofuns, notably the oldest such mound in Kyushu, the Akatsuka Kofun, along with surrounding Kofuns. It is situated on a plateau on the right bank of the {{ill|Ekigawa River|ja|駅館川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekigawa River|1=Yakkan River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668226}}, which runs north-south through Usa City. Within the park is the central exhibit and research facility, the '''[[:en:Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History]]'''. This museum was originally established as the Ōita Prefectural Usa Fudoki no Oka Historical Folklore Museum at the same time as the park’s opening. In 1998 (Heisei 10), it underwent expansion to include a permanent exhibition hall and was renamed the Ōita Prefectural Museum of History, which it remains today.https://www.city.usa.oita.jp/tourist/touristspot/touristspot2/touristspot3/10184.html Additionally, various seasonal flowers and trees are planted throughout the park, with cherry blossoms in April and irises in June being particularly famous as scenic highlights. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History]] * [[:en:Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster]] == External Links == * [http://rekisihakubutukan-b.oita-ed.jp/rekihaku/usafudoki.html Historic Site Park "Usa Fudoki no Oka"] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|宇佐風土記の丘|version=97995589|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:宇佐風土記の丘]] [[ja:%E5%AE%87%E4%BD%90%E9%A2%A8%E5%9C%9F%E8%A8%98%E3%81%AE%E4%B8%98]] [[Category:National historic sites in Oita prefecture]] [[Category:Parks and gardens in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Usa, Oita]] [[Category:History of Usa City]] {{wikidata link|Q11449110}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q11449110}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

In 1998 (Heisei 10), it underwent expansion to include a permanent exhibition hall and was renamed the Ōita Prefectural Museum of History, which it remains today.https://www.city.usa.oita.jp/tourist/touristspot/touristspot2/touristspot3/10184.html Additionally, various seasonal flowers and trees are planted throughout the park, with cherry blossoms in April and irises in June being particularly famous as scenic highlights. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|en|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|WD=Q3329680|lt=Ōita Prefectural Museum of History}} * {{ill|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|en|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11479328|lt=Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster}} == External Links == * [http://rekisihakubutukan-b.oita-ed.jp/rekihaku/usafudoki.html Historic Site Park "Usa Fudoki no Oka"] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|宇佐風土記の丘|version=97995589|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:宇佐風土記の丘]] [[ja:%E5%AE%87%E4%BD%90%E9%A2%A8%E5%9C%9F%E8%A8%98%E3%81%AE%E4%B8%98]] {{wikidata link|Q11449110}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Geography of Usa, Oita]] [[Category:History of Usa City]] [[Category:National historic sites in Oita prefecture]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Parks and gardens in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]]

About 5 minutes by car from JR Yanagigaura Station
About 5 minutes by car from JR Buzen-Nagasu Station |official_site = [https://www.pref.oita.jp/site/rekishihakubutsukan/ Ōita Prefectural Board of Education Official Page] }} '''Usa Fudoki no Oka''' (宇佐風土記の丘) is an {{ill|Open-air museum|en|Open-air museum|WD=Q756102|lt=open-air museum}} and historic site {{ill|Park|en|Park|WD=Q22698|lt=park}} located in Kyōzuka, Takamori, {{ill|Usa, Ōita|en|Usa, Ōita|WD=Q114147|lt=Usa, Ōita}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture}}, established in 1981 (Shōwa 56) based on the {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en|Agency for Cultural Affairs|WD=Q1009519|lt=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}'s plan for creating "Fudoki no Oka." == Overview == The park covers an extensive area of 19.2 hectares, centered around the {{ill|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|en|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11479328|lt=Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster}}, a nationally designated {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} that includes six keyhole-shaped Kofuns, notably the oldest such mound in Kyushu, the Akatsuka Kofun, along with surrounding Kofuns. It is situated on a plateau on the right bank of the {{ill|Ekigawa River|ja|駅館川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekigawa River|1=Yakkan River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668226}}, which runs north-south through Usa City. Within the park is the central exhibit and research facility, the '''{{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|en|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|WD=Q3329680|lt=Ōita Prefectural Museum of History}}'''. This museum was originally established as the Ōita Prefectural Usa Fudoki no Oka Historical Folklore Museum at the same time as the park’s opening. In 1998 (Heisei 10), it underwent expansion to include a permanent exhibition hall and was renamed the Ōita Prefectural Museum of History, which it remains today.https://www.city.usa.oita.jp/tourist/touristspot/touristspot2/touristspot3/10184.html Additionally, various seasonal flowers and trees are planted throughout the park, with cherry blossoms in April and irises in June being particularly famous as scenic highlights. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|en|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|WD=Q3329680|lt=Ōita Prefectural Museum of History}} * {{ill|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|en|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11479328|lt=Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster}} == External Links == * [http://rekisihakubutukan-b.oita-ed.jp/rekihaku/usafudoki.html Historic Site Park "Usa Fudoki no Oka"] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|宇佐風土記の丘|version=97995589|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:宇佐風土記の丘]] [[ja:%E5%AE%87%E4%BD%90%E9%A2%A8%E5%9C%9F%E8%A8%98%E3%81%AE%E4%B8%98]] {{wikidata link|Q11449110}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Geography of Usa, Oita]] [[Category:History of Usa City]] [[Category:National historic sites in Oita prefecture]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Parks and gardens in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]]

About 5 minutes by car from JR Buzen-Nagasu Station |official_site = [https://www.pref.oita.jp/site/rekishihakubutsukan/ Ōita Prefectural Board of Education Official Page] }} '''Usa Fudoki no Oka''' (宇佐風土記の丘) is an {{ill|Open-air museum|en|Open-air museum|WD=Q756102|lt=open-air museum|1=Open-air museum}} and historic site {{ill|Park|en|Park|WD=Q22698|lt=park|1=Park}} located in Kyōzuka, Takamori, {{ill|Usa, Ōita|en|Usa, Ōita|WD=Q114147|lt=Usa, Ōita|1=Usa, Ōita}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture|1=Ōita Prefecture}}, established in 1981 (Shōwa 56) based on the {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en|Agency for Cultural Affairs|WD=Q1009519|lt=Agency for Cultural Affairs|1=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}'s plan for creating "Fudoki no Oka." == Overview == The park covers an extensive area of 19.2 hectares, centered around the {{ill|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|en|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11479328|lt=Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|1=Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster}}, a nationally designated {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan|1=Historic site}}} that includes six keyhole-shaped Kofuns, notably the oldest such mound in Kyushu, the Akatsuka Kofun, along with surrounding Kofuns. It is situated on a plateau on the right bank of the {{ill|Ekigawa River|ja|駅館川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekigawa River|1=Yakkan River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668226}}, which runs north-south through Usa City. Within the park is the central exhibit and research facility, the '''{{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|en|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|WD=Q3329680|lt=Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|1=Ōita Prefectural Museum of History}}'''. This museum was originally established as the Ōita Prefectural Usa Fudoki no Oka Historical Folklore Museum at the same time as the park’s opening. In 1998 (Heisei 10), it underwent expansion to include a permanent exhibition hall and was renamed the Ōita Prefectural Museum of History, which it remains today.https://www.city.usa.oita.jp/tourist/touristspot/touristspot2/touristspot3/10184.html Additionally, various seasonal flowers and trees are planted throughout the park, with cherry blossoms in April and irises in June being particularly famous as scenic highlights. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|en|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|WD=Q3329680|lt=Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|1=Ōita Prefectural Museum of History}} * {{ill|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|en|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11479328|lt=Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|1=Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster}} == External Links == * [http://rekisihakubutukan-b.oita-ed.jp/rekihaku/usafudoki.html Historic Site Park "Usa Fudoki no Oka"] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|宇佐風土記の丘|version=97995589|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:宇佐風土記の丘]] [[ja:%E5%AE%87%E4%BD%90%E9%A2%A8%E5%9C%9F%E8%A8%98%E3%81%AE%E4%B8%98]] {{wikidata link|Q11449110}} [[Category:Geography of Usa, Oita]] [[Category:History of Usa City]] [[Category:National historic sites in Oita prefecture]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Parks and gardens in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]] [[Category:pages]]

About 5 minutes by car from JR Buzen-Nagasu Station |official_site = [https://www.pref.oita.jp/site/rekishihakubutsukan/ Ōita Prefectural Board of Education Official Page] }} '''Usa Fudoki no Oka''' (宇佐風土記の丘) is an {{ill|Open-air museum|en|Open-air museum|WD=Q756102|lt=open-air museum|1=Open-air museum}} and historic site {{ill|Park|en|Park|WD=Q22698|lt=park|1=Park}} located in Kyōzuka, Takamori, {{ill|Usa, Ōita|en|Usa, Ōita|WD=Q114147|lt=Usa, Ōita|1=Usa, Ōita}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture|1=Ōita Prefecture}}, established in 1981 (Shōwa 56) based on the {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en|Agency for Cultural Affairs|WD=Q1009519|lt=Agency for Cultural Affairs|1=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}'s plan for creating "Fudoki no Oka." == Overview == The park covers an extensive area of 19.2 hectares, centered around the {{ill|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|en|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11479328|lt=Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|1=Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster}}, a nationally designated {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan|1=Historic site}}} that includes six keyhole-shaped Kofuns, notably the oldest such mound in Kyushu, the Akatsuka Kofun, along with surrounding Kofuns. It is situated on a plateau on the right bank of the {{ill|Ekigawa River|ja|駅館川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekigawa River|1=Yakkan River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668226}}, which runs north-south through Usa City. Within the park is the central exhibit and research facility, the '''{{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|en|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|WD=Q3329680|lt=Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|1=Ōita Prefectural Museum of History}}'''. This museum was originally established as the Ōita Prefectural Usa Fudoki no Oka Historical Folklore Museum at the same time as the park’s opening. In 1998 (Heisei 10), it underwent expansion to include a permanent exhibition hall and was renamed the Ōita Prefectural Museum of History, which it remains today.https://www.city.usa.oita.jp/tourist/touristspot/touristspot2/touristspot3/10184.html Additionally, various seasonal flowers and trees are planted throughout the park, with cherry blossoms in April and irises in June being particularly famous as scenic highlights. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|en|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|WD=Q3329680|lt=Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|1=Ōita Prefectural Museum of History}} * {{ill|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|en|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11479328|lt=Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|1=Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster}} == External Links == * [http://rekisihakubutukan-b.oita-ed.jp/rekihaku/usafudoki.html Historic Site Park "Usa Fudoki no Oka"] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|宇佐風土記の丘|version=97995589|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11449110|ja|宇佐風土記の丘|ja|%E5%AE%87%E4%BD%90%E9%A2%A8%E5%9C%9F%E8%A8%98%E3%81%AE%E4%B8%98}} [[Category:Geography of Usa, Oita]] [[Category:History of Usa City]] [[Category:National historic sites in Oita prefecture]] [[Category:Parks and gardens in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with interwikis with duplicate languages]]
